trivia_bullets.text: Modern Horizons 3 (released June 14, 2024) is a draft-innovation set designed to inject powerful cards directly into Modern and Commander without passing through Standard. MH3 organized its draft archetypes around three major themes: Energy, Eldrazi, and Modified. Glyph Elemental sits in the Modified-adjacent white slot, where +1/+1 counters and Aura synergies reinforce the Bestow-Landfall engine. [Scryfall: set_type draft_innovation, released_at 2024-06-14]

Glyph Elemental is an Enchantment Creature — Elemental with dual identity via Bestow {1}{W}: cast as a creature it grows itself on Landfall; cast as an Aura it funnels those counters to the enchanted creature instead. MTGAZone's MH3 white limited review rated it 3.5/5, noting that cheap Bestow creatures are unusually resilient in limited formats because they negate most of the risk of playing Auras — the enchanted creature dying simply leaves a live 2/2 body that continues accruing counters. Its EDHREC rank of 9,340 reflects modest Commander traction; it is a draft-role card rather than a constructed pillar. [MTGAZone MH3 White Limited Set Review; Scryfall EDHREC rank]
sources: MTGAZone: Modern Horizons 3 MH3 Limited Set Review White

trivia_bullets.text: Glyph Elemental is a first printing with no reprint history as of 2025. Art credit: Domenico Cava. The card carries the enchantment-frame aesthetic (sunburst border treatment) matching its Enchantment Creature type line, a frame effect active in MH3's white enchantment-creature cycle. [Scryfall: frame_effects enchantment, artist Domenico Cava]
